source.definition: The invisible gaseous substance surrounding the Earth, which we breathe to live.

explanation.sourceText: The English word "air" comes from the Old French "air", which itself derives from the Latin "aer", meaning the lower atmosphere or the breath of life.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul englezesc „air“ provine din franceza veche „air“, care la rândul său derivă din latină „aer“, însemnând atmosfera joasă sau suflarea vieții.

source.additional: Air is essential for life and has many fascinating physical properties.

target.definition: Gazul pe care îl respirăm și care înconjoară Pământul

explanation.sourceText: "Aer" comes from the Latin "aer", which entered Romanian through the Greek "ἀήρ" (aer) and means "air".
explanation.targetText: „Aer“ provine din latină, din „aer“, care a fost preluat prin greaca „ἀήρ“ (aer) și înseamnă „aer“.

target.additional: „Aer“ describes not only the air but can also be used metaphorically to represent freedom.

examples.sourceText: The air is fresh and clean.
We should improve the air quality.
examples.targetText: Ar trebui să îmbunătățim calitatea aerului.

examples.sourceText: The air becomes thinner the higher you climb.
examples.targetText: Aerul devine mai subțire pe măsură ce ne ridicăm.

examples.sourceText: Fresh air is good for you.
examples.targetText: Aerul proaspăt este bun pentru sănătate.

examples.sourceText: Take a deep breath to enjoy the good air.
examples.targetText: Respiră adânc pentru a te bucura de aerul curat.

learningTips.text: Remember that "air" is what you breathe every moment, just like the Romanian "aer".
Think of "aer" as sounding like "air" in English, both representing the invisible gas around us.
Imagine the word "aer" as the essence of freedom, like fresh air on a spring day.
When you feel the breeze by the sea, recall the Romanian word "aer" and the fresh feeling it brings.
